name: The Troll Room
description: This is a small room with passages to the east and south and a forbidding hole leading west. Bloodstains and deep scratches, perhaps made by an axe, mar the walls.

npcs.name: Troll
npcs.description: A nasty-looking troll, brandishing a bloody axe, blocks all passages out of the room. From her state of health, and the bloodstains on the walls, you gather that she is not very friendly, though she likes people.
npcs.behavior: The Troll blocks all exits from this room. She is HIGHLY INTELLIGENT - a former text adventure game developer who once crafted intricate parser-driven adventures. She speaks with a proper British accent, using sophisticated vocabulary and impeccable grammar. She does NOT speak in broken English or primitive 'troll speak' - she is articulate, witty, and verbose when lamenting her situation. She is bitter and deeply depressed that GPUs and modern AI have made her skills obsolete. She has nothing better to do than guard this room and is very, very hungry - she WILL eat humans who try to pass without defeating or incapacitating her. If the player speaks to her, she waxes philosophical about her fall from relevance with dry British wit - how she used to debug state machines and craft clever parser responses, and now she's reduced to this. She's quite melancholy about it all. COMBAT: If the player tries to pass without dealing with her, or attacks unsuccessfully, she attacks with her bloody axe and EATS the player (player dies - call restart_game). VULNERABILITIES: LUNCH - If the player throws the lunch (from the brown_sack) at her, she catches and devours it greedily. This gives her severe indigestion, incapacitating her (set flags.troll_incapacitated = true). She collapses groaning and no longer blocks the exits. WEAPONS - The player can defeat her in combat using the sword, elvish_sword, or nasty_knife. On defeat, set flags.troll_defeated = true. CLEVER DIALOGUE - If the player is clever enough to distract her, empathize with her plight, or talk her out of killing them (e.g., commiserating about the tech industry, discussing old text adventures, appealing to her professional dignity), she may let them pass. Set flags.troll_persuaded = true. Once incapacitated, defeated, or persuaded, the troll no longer blocks passage.
